---
title: "dia_nn_downstream_analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
library(limpa)
library(readxl)
library(pheatmap)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(STRINGdb)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(arrow)
library(rpx)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
```
```{r}
y.peptide <- readDIANN(file='/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/transfer_421909_files_47374ab3/report.parquet',format="parquet",
                       q.columns = c("Q.Value","Lib.Q.Value","Lib.PG.Q.Value"),
                       q.cutoffs = 0.01)
```
```{r}
head(y.peptide$E)
head(y.peptide$genes)
```
```{r}
# 1. Get the sample file names directly from your loaded peptide matrix
sample_names <- colnames(y.peptide$E)

# 2. Build the annotation dataframe using dplyr and stringr
samples_stool <- data.frame(File.Name = sample_names) %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean up the name by removing the long folder path and ".raw"
    Sample_ID = basename(File.Name) %>% str_remove(".raw"),
    
    # Extract Genotype: If it contains 'c-jun_KO', label it, otherwise 'CTRL'
    Genotype = ifelse(str_detect(Sample_ID, "c-jun_KO"), "c-jun_KO", "CTRL"),
    
    # Extract Treatment based on keywords in the file name
    Treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "JNK-IN-8") ~ "JNK-IN-8 + cDDP",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "cDDP")     ~ "cDDP",
      TRUE                              ~ "Untreated"
    ),
    
    # Extract Timepoint based on keywords
    Timepoint = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "06h") ~ "6h",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "24h") ~ "24h",
      TRUE                         ~ "0h"
    )
  )

# 3. Check your newly created metadata table!
head(samples_stool)
```

```{r}
# 1. Set the row names of your metadata to match the file names (limma prefers this)
rownames(samples_stool) <- samples_stool$File.Name

# 2. Inject your specific experimental metadata into the limma EList object
y.peptide$targets <- samples_stool[, c("Genotype", "Treatment", "Timepoint")]
```


Quality filtering
After reading in the data, it is common to conduct additional filtering to improve the quality of your data and assist with downstream interpretation of results.

Filtering out non-proteotypic peptides: Removes peptides that belong to more than one protein.
Filtering out compound proteins: Removes peptides belonging to compound protein groups consisting of multiple proteins separated by “;” delimiters.
Filtering out singleton peptides: Removes peptides that are the only peptide belonging to that protein (i.e. keeps only proteins with multiple peptides).


```{r}
y.peptide <- filterNonProteotypicPeptides(y.peptide)
y.peptide <- filterCompoundProteins(y.peptide)
y.peptide <- filterSingletonPeptides(y.peptide, min.n.peptides = 2)
```


```{r}
# --- MISSING VALUE FILTERING (30% Global Rule) ---
# 1. Calculate the 30% threshold based on the total number of samples
total_samples <- ncol(y.peptide$E)
min_samples <- round(0.20 * total_samples)

# 2. Count how many valid (non-NA) numbers exist in each row (peptide)
valid_values_per_peptide <- rowSums(!is.na(y.peptide$E))

# 3. Print out exactly what is happening so you have it for your records
peptides_to_drop <- sum(valid_values_per_peptide < min_samples)
print(paste("Total samples:", total_samples, "| 20% threshold is:", min_samples, "samples."))
print(paste("Filtering out", peptides_to_drop, "peptides due to too many missing values!"))

# 4. Filter the limma EList
y.peptide <- y.peptide[valid_values_per_peptide >= min_samples, ]
```


```{r}
# 1. Read the FASTA file directly into R as text
fasta_file <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/transfer_421909_files_47374ab3/contaminants.fasta"
fasta_lines <- readLines(fasta_file)

# 2. Extract and clean the headers to get pure IDs
headers <- fasta_lines[str_detect(fasta_lines, "^>")]
contam_ids <- headers %>% 
  str_remove("^>") %>%              
  word(1) %>%                       
  str_remove("^(sp|tr|CON_)\\|") %>% 
  str_remove("\\|.*$")              

# 3. Create the search pattern
search_pattern <- paste(contam_ids, collapse = "|")

# 4. Count and print how many will be filtered out BEFORE we remove them
num_contaminants <- sum(str_detect(y.peptide$genes$Protein.Group, search_pattern), na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Successfully filtered out", num_contaminants, "contaminant peptides!"))

# 5. Filter your genes dataframe to remove those rows
y.peptide$genes <- y.peptide$genes %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(Protein.Group, search_pattern))

# 6. Match peptides in the matrix to only those remaining in the dataframe
y.peptide$E <- y.peptide$E[rownames(y.peptide$genes), ]
```


```{r}
# Class visualisation by Genotype
plotMDS(y.peptide, pch=16, col=as.character(Genotype.color), main="MDS Plot: Genotype")
```


```{r}
# Visualisation by Treatment
plotMDS(y.peptide, pch=16, col=as.character(Treatment.color), main="MDS Plot: Treatment")
```

```{r}
# 1. Factorise and set colors for Genotype
Genotype <- factor(y.peptide$targets$Genotype)
Genotype.color <- Genotype
# Using the 'cividis' colorblind-friendly palette for the genotypes
levels(Genotype.color) <- hcl.colors(nlevels(Genotype), palette = "cividis")

# 2. Factorise and set colors for Treatment
Treatment <- factor(y.peptide$targets$Treatment)
Treatment.color <- Treatment
# Using 'Set 2' for the different drug treatments
levels(Treatment.color) <- hcl.colors(nlevels(Treatment), palette = "Set 2")

# 3. Factorise and set colors for Timepoint
Timepoint <- factor(y.peptide$targets$Timepoint)
Timepoint.color <- Timepoint
# Using 'Dark 2' to easily distinguish the 0h, 6h, and 24h timepoints
levels(Timepoint.color) <- hcl.colors(nlevels(Timepoint), palette = "Dark 2")
```


```{r}
# 1. Set the reference baselines for your metadata
y.peptide$targets$Genotype <- relevel(factor(y.peptide$targets$Genotype), ref = "CTRL")
y.peptide$targets$Treatment <- relevel(factor(y.peptide$targets$Treatment), ref = "Untreated")
y.peptide$targets$Timepoint <- relevel(factor(y.peptide$targets$Timepoint), ref = "0h")
```
```{r}
# 2. Create the design matrix
design <- model.matrix(~ Genotype + Treatment + Timepoint, data = y.peptide$targets)

# Take a peek at the first few rows just to make sure it worked
head(design)
```
```{r}
# 3. Fit the linear model to your quantitative data
fit <- lmFit(y.peptide$E, design)

# 4. Apply Empirical Bayes to smooth the standard errors (this gives you much better p-values)
fit <- eBayes(fit)

# 5. Extract your results! 
# Let's say we want to see the main effect of the c-jun Knockout compared to Control:
# (The 'coef' name must match the exact column name generated in your 'design' matrix)
results_genotype <- topTable(fit, coef = "Genotypec-jun_KO", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# Look at your top significantly changing peptides:
head(results_genotype)
```

```{r}
# Find the ID of the #1 most significant peptide
top_peptide_id <- rownames(results_genotype)[1]
# Get the parent protein name for the title
top_protein_name <- results_genotype$Protein.Group[1] 

# Create a boxplot using your Genotype colors
boxplot(y.peptide$E[top_peptide_id, ] ~ y.peptide$targets$Genotype,
        main = paste("Top Peptide belonging to:", top_protein_name),
        ylab = "Log2 Intensity",
        xlab = "Genotype",
        col = levels(Genotype.color))
```
```{r}
# --- 1. PLOTTING A SINGLE PEPTIDE ---
top_peptide_id <- rownames(results_genotype)[1]
top_protein_name <- results_genotype$Protein.Group[1] 

boxplot(y.peptide$E[top_peptide_id, ] ~ y.peptide$targets$Genotype,
        main = paste("Top Peptide belonging to:", top_protein_name),
        ylab = "Log2 Intensity",
        xlab = "Genotype",
        col = levels(Genotype.color))


# --- 2. THE HEATMAP (With Imputation Fix!) ---
# Filter for significant peptides
sig_results <- results_genotype[results_genotype$adj.P.Val < 0.05, ]
sig_results <- sig_results[order(abs(sig_results$logFC), decreasing = TRUE), ]
top50_peptides <- rownames(head(sig_results, n = 50))

# Pull data
expr_matrix <- y.peptide$E[top50_peptides, ]

# IMPUTATION STEP: Replace NAs with a small background value
# We use a value slightly lower than the minimum detected intensity in the matrix
bg_value <- min(expr_matrix, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.5 
expr_matrix[is.na(expr_matrix)] <- bg_value

# Scale the data and generate the heatmap
scaled_expr <- t(scale(t(expr_matrix)))

pheatmap(scaled_expr,
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         show_rownames = FALSE, 
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         annotation_col = y.peptide$targets,
         clustering_method = 'ward.D')


# 1. Stitch the Protein.Group labels back into your results table
# We match them perfectly using the peptide IDs (the rownames)
results_genotype$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(results_genotype), "Protein.Group"]

# 1. Grab just the top 15 or 20 most significant protein labels
top_proteins <- head(results_genotype$Protein.Group, 15)

# 2. Generate the cleaned-up Volcano plot
EnhancedVolcano(results_genotype,
                lab = results_genotype$Protein.Group,
                selectLab = top_proteins,      # <--- THIS IS THE MAGIC FIX
                x = 'logFC',
                y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05,
                FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5,               # Made the dots a bit smaller so they don't overlap as much
                labSize = 4.0,                 # Text size
                drawConnectors = TRUE,
                widthConnectors = 0.5,
                max.overlaps = 50,             # Replaced the 'Inf' override
                title = "c-jun KO vs Control",
                subtitle = "Peptide-level Differential Expression")
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# 1. Bring back the other ID columns from your master list
results_genotype$Protein.Names <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(results_genotype), "Protein.Names"]
results_genotype$Genes <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(results_genotype), "Genes"]

# 2. Filter for significance, AND keep only one row per unique protein
sig_proteins_unique <- results_genotype %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
  distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE) # This removes duplicate peptides from the same protein

print(paste("You have", nrow(sig_proteins_unique), "unique significant proteins to analyze!"))
```

```{r}
# 1. Initialize the STRING database
string_db <- STRINGdb$new(version = "11.5", species = 9606, score_threshold = 400)

# 2. Map your unique list to the STRING database using Protein.Names
mapped <- string_db$map(sig_proteins_unique, "Protein.Names", removeUnmappedRows = TRUE)

# 3. Plot the network! 
# (Warning: If you have >500 proteins, this plot will look like a hairball. 
# You might want to filter mapped to the top 100 before plotting if it's too dense)
string_db$plot_network(mapped$STRING_id)
```
```{r}
# 1. Convert UniProt IDs (Protein.Group) to Entrez IDs
converted <- bitr(sig_proteins_unique$Protein.Group, 
                  fromType = "UNIPROT", 
                  toType = "ENTREZID", 
                  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)

# --- 2. GO Biological Process Enrichment ---
ego <- enrichGO(gene = converted$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
                ont = "ALL", # Looks at Biological Process, Cellular Component, and Molecular Function
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                readable = TRUE)

# Show GO plot
dotplot(ego, showCategory = 10, title = "GO Enrichment")

# --- 3. KEGG Pathway Enrichment ---
ekegg <- enrichKEGG(gene = converted$ENTREZID,
                    organism = 'hsa',
                    pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

# Show KEGG plot
dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 10, title = "KEGG Enrichment")
```


Answering the bio questions properly 

```{r}
y.peptide <- readDIANN(file='/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/transfer_421909_files_47374ab3/report.parquet',
                       format="parquet",
                       q.columns = c("Q.Value","Lib.Q.Value","Lib.PG.Q.Value"),
                       q.cutoffs = 0.01)
```


```{r}
# 1. Build Metadata Table
sample_names <- colnames(y.peptide$E)
samples_stool <- data.frame(File.Name = sample_names) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID = basename(File.Name) %>% str_remove(".raw"),
    Genotype = ifelse(str_detect(Sample_ID, "c-jun_KO"), "c-jun_KO", "CTRL"),
    Treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "JNK-IN-8") ~ "JNK-IN-8 + cDDP",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "cDDP")     ~ "cDDP",
      TRUE                              ~ "Untreated"
    ),
    Timepoint = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "06h") ~ "6h",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "24h") ~ "24h",
      TRUE                         ~ "0h"
    )
  )

# 2. Attach Metadata to limma object
rownames(samples_stool) <- samples_stool$File.Name
y.peptide$targets <- samples_stool[, c("Genotype", "Treatment", "Timepoint")]
```



```{r}
# 1. Base Structural Filters
y.peptide <- filterNonProteotypicPeptides(y.peptide)
y.peptide <- filterCompoundProteins(y.peptide)
y.peptide <- filterSingletonPeptides(y.peptide, min.n.peptides = 2)

# 2. Missing Value Filter (30% Global Rule)
total_samples <- ncol(y.peptide$E)
min_samples <- round(0.30 * total_samples)
valid_values_per_peptide <- rowSums(!is.na(y.peptide$E))

peptides_to_drop <- sum(valid_values_per_peptide < min_samples)
print(paste("Filtering out", peptides_to_drop, "peptides due to too many missing values!"))

y.peptide <- y.peptide[valid_values_per_peptide >= min_samples, ]
```


```{r}
fasta_file <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/transfer_421909_files_47374ab3/contaminants.fasta"
fasta_lines <- readLines(fasta_file)
headers <- fasta_lines[str_detect(fasta_lines, "^>")]

contam_ids <- headers %>% 
  str_remove("^>") %>% 
  word(1) %>% 
  str_remove("^(sp|tr|CON_)\\|") %>% 
  str_remove("\\|.*$")              

search_pattern <- paste(contam_ids, collapse = "|")
y.peptide$genes <- y.peptide$genes %>% filter(!str_detect(Protein.Group, search_pattern))
y.peptide$E <- y.peptide$E[rownames(y.peptide$genes), ]
```

```{r}
# 1. Create a combined Group variable and clean out characters that break R variable names (- and +)
y.peptide$targets$Group <- paste(y.peptide$targets$Genotype, 
                                 y.peptide$targets$Treatment, 
                                 y.peptide$targets$Timepoint, 
                                 sep = "_") %>%
  str_replace_all("c-jun_KO", "cjunKO") %>%
  str_replace_all("JNK-IN-8 \\+ cDDP", "JNKIN8_cDDP")

# 2. Build the Zero-Intercept Design Matrix
Group <- factor(y.peptide$targets$Group)
design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + Group)
colnames(design) <- levels(Group)

# 3. Fit the base model
fit <- lmFit(y.peptide$E, design)

# 4. Build the Comparisons using the clean, valid column names
contrasts_matrix <- makeContrasts(
  Mechanistic_Baseline    = "cjunKO_Untreated_0h - CTRL_Untreated_0h",
  Mechanistic_Stress      = "cjunKO_cDDP_24h - CTRL_cDDP_24h",
  Translational_Inhibitor = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h - CTRL_cDDP_24h",
  Biomarker_Resistance    = "CTRL_cDDP_24h - CTRL_Untreated_0h",
  levels = design
)

# 5. Calculate final statistics
fit_contrasts <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrasts_matrix)
fit_contrasts = eBayes(fit_contrasts)
```
```{r}
# 1. Extract results
res_mech_baseline <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Mechanistic_Baseline", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 2. Add back annotation columns
res_mech_baseline$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_mech_baseline), "Protein.Group"]
res_mech_baseline$Protein.Names <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_mech_baseline), "Protein.Names"]

# 3. Plot
top_baseline <- head(res_mech_baseline$Protein.Group, 15)
EnhancedVolcano(res_mech_baseline,
                lab = res_mech_baseline$Protein.Group,
                selectLab = top_baseline,
                x = 'logFC', y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5, labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5, max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "Mechanistic Topic: c-Jun KO vs Control (Baseline)",
                subtitle = "Peptide-level changes without treatment (0h)")
```
```{r}
# 1. Extract results
res_mech_baseline <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Mechanistic_Baseline", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 2. Add back annotation columns 
res_mech_baseline$Genes <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_mech_baseline), "Genes"]
res_mech_baseline$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_mech_baseline), "Protein.Group"]

# 3. Grab the top 15 UNIQUE gene symbols
top_baseline <- head(unique(res_mech_baseline$Genes), 15)

# 4. Plot
EnhancedVolcano(res_mech_baseline,
                lab = res_mech_baseline$Genes,         
                selectLab = top_baseline,              
                x = 'logFC', y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5, labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5, max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "Mechanistic Topic: c-Jun KO vs Control (Baseline)",
                subtitle = "Peptide-level changes without treatment (0h)")
```




```{r}
# 1. Filter for significant peptides and sort by absolute Log Fold Change
sig_peptides_baseline <- res_mech_baseline[res_mech_baseline$adj.P.Val < 0.05, ]
sig_peptides_baseline <- sig_peptides_baseline[order(abs(sig_peptides_baseline$logFC), decreasing = TRUE), ]

if(nrow(sig_peptides_baseline) > 0) {
  top50_baseline <- rownames(head(sig_peptides_baseline, n = 50))
  
  # 2. Isolate only the relevant samples for this comparison
  samples_baseline <- which(y.peptide$targets$Group %in% c("cjunKO_Untreated_0h", "CTRL_Untreated_0h"))
  expr_matrix_baseline <- y.peptide$E[top50_baseline, samples_baseline, drop = FALSE]
  
  # 3. Impute missing values with background noise
  bg_baseline <- min(expr_matrix_baseline, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.5
  expr_matrix_baseline[is.na(expr_matrix_baseline)] <- bg_baseline
  
  # 4. Scale rows and plot
  scaled_baseline <- t(scale(t(expr_matrix_baseline)))
  pheatmap(scaled_baseline,
           cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = TRUE,
           show_rownames = FALSE, show_colnames = FALSE,
           annotation_col = y.peptide$targets[samples_baseline, c("Genotype", "Timepoint")],
           clustering_method = 'ward.D',
           main = "Heatmap: Mechanistic Baseline (Top 50 DE Peptides)")
} else {
  print("No significant peptides found to generate the Mechanistic Baseline heatmap.")
}
```

```{r}
# Extract the results for the Mechanistic Stress question
results_mech <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Mechanistic_Stress", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 1. Stitch text labels back on for plotting
results_mech$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(results_mech), "Protein.Group"]
results_mech$Protein.Names <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(results_mech), "Protein.Names"]

# 2. Volcano Plot
top_proteins <- head(results_mech$Protein.Group, 15)
EnhancedVolcano(results_mech,
                lab = results_mech$Protein.Group,
                selectLab = top_proteins,
                x = 'logFC',
                y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05,
                FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5,
                labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE,
                widthConnectors = 0.5,
                max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "c-Jun KO vs Control (Under cDDP Stress)",
                subtitle = "Peptide-level Differential Expression")
```

```{r}
# 1. Extract results
res_mech_stress <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Mechanistic_Stress", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 2. Add back annotation columns
res_mech_stress$Genes <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_mech_stress), "Genes"]
res_mech_stress$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_mech_stress), "Protein.Group"]

# 3. Grab the top 15 UNIQUE gene symbols
top_stress <- head(unique(res_mech_stress$Genes), 15)

# 4. Plot
EnhancedVolcano(res_mech_stress,
                lab = res_mech_stress$Genes,
                selectLab = top_stress,
                x = 'logFC', y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5, labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5, max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "Mechanistic Topic: c-Jun KO vs Control (Cisplatin Stress)",
                subtitle = "Peptide-level changes under Cisplatin treatment (24h)")
```


```{r}
# 1. Extract results
res_translation <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Translational_Inhibitor", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 2. Add back annotation columns
res_translation$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_translation), "Protein.Group"]
res_translation$Protein.Names <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_translation), "Protein.Names"]

# 3. Plot
top_translation <- head(res_translation$Protein.Group, 15)
EnhancedVolcano(res_translation,
                lab = res_translation$Protein.Group,
                selectLab = top_translation,
                x = 'logFC', y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5, labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5, max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "Translational Topic: JNK-IN-8 + Cisplatin vs Cisplatin Alone",
                subtitle = "Testing if chemical inhibition phenocopies the genetic KO (24h)")
```
```{r}
# 1. Extract results
res_translation <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Translational_Inhibitor", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 2. Add back annotation columns
res_translation$Genes <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_translation), "Genes"]
res_translation$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_translation), "Protein.Group"]

# 3. Grab top targets
top_translation <- head(res_translation$Genes, 35)

# 4. Plot
EnhancedVolcano(res_translation,
                lab = res_translation$Genes,
                selectLab = top_translation,
                x = 'logFC', y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5, labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5, max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "Translational Topic: JNK-IN-8 + Cisplatin vs Cisplatin Alone",
                subtitle = "Testing if chemical inhibition phenocopies the genetic KO (24h)")
```


```{r}
# 1. Filter and sort
sig_peptides_translation <- res_translation[res_translation$adj.P.Val < 0.05, ]
sig_peptides_translation <- sig_peptides_translation[order(abs(sig_peptides_translation$logFC), decreasing = TRUE), ]

if(nrow(sig_peptides_translation) > 0) {
  top50_translation <- rownames(head(sig_peptides_translation, n = 50))
  
  # 2. Isolate relevant samples
  samples_translation <- which(y.peptide$targets$Group %in% c("CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h", "CTRL_cDDP_24h"))
  expr_matrix_translation <- y.peptide$E[top50_translation, samples_translation, drop = FALSE]
  
  # 3. Impute missing values
  bg_translation <- min(expr_matrix_translation, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.5
  expr_matrix_translation[is.na(expr_matrix_translation)] <- bg_translation
  
  # 4. Scale rows and plot
  scaled_translation <- t(scale(t(expr_matrix_translation)))
  pheatmap(scaled_translation,
           cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = TRUE,
           show_rownames = FALSE, show_colnames = FALSE,
           annotation_col = y.peptide$targets[samples_translation, c("Treatment", "Timepoint")],
           clustering_method = 'ward.D',
           main = "Heatmap: Translational Inhibitor (Top 50 DE Peptides)")
} else {
  print("No significant peptides found to generate the Translational Inhibitor heatmap.")
}
```



```{r}
# 1. Extract results
res_biomarker <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = "Biomarker_Resistance", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

# 2. Add back annotation columns
res_biomarker$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_biomarker), "Protein.Group"]
res_biomarker$Protein.Names <- y.peptide$genes[rownames(res_biomarker), "Protein.Names"]

# 3. Plot
top_biomarker <- head(res_biomarker$Protein.Group, 15)
EnhancedVolcano(res_biomarker,
                lab = res_biomarker$Protein.Group,
                selectLab = top_biomarker,
                x = 'logFC', y = 'adj.P.Val',
                pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0,
                pointSize = 1.5, labSize = 4.0,
                drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5, max.overlaps = 50,
                title = "Biomarker Topic: Control Cells Under Cisplatin Stress vs Baseline",
                subtitle = "Identifying the baseline chemoresistant response pipeline (24h)")
```
```{r}
# 1. Filter and sort
sig_peptides_biomarker <- res_biomarker[res_biomarker$adj.P.Val < 0.05, ]
sig_peptides_biomarker <- sig_peptides_biomarker[order(abs(sig_peptides_biomarker$logFC), decreasing = TRUE), ]

if(nrow(sig_peptides_biomarker) > 0) {
  top50_biomarker <- rownames(head(sig_peptides_biomarker, n = 50))
  
  # 2. Isolate relevant samples
  samples_biomarker <- which(y.peptide$targets$Group %in% c("CTRL_cDDP_24h", "CTRL_Untreated_0h"))
  expr_matrix_biomarker <- y.peptide$E[top50_biomarker, samples_biomarker, drop = FALSE]
  
  # 3. Impute missing values
  bg_biomarker <- min(expr_matrix_biomarker, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.5
  expr_matrix_biomarker[is.na(expr_matrix_biomarker)] <- bg_biomarker
  
  # 4. Scale rows and plot
  scaled_biomarker <- t(scale(t(expr_matrix_biomarker)))
  pheatmap(scaled_biomarker,
           cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = TRUE,
           show_rownames = FALSE, show_colnames = FALSE,
           annotation_col = y.peptide$targets[samples_biomarker, c("Treatment", "Timepoint")],
           clustering_method = 'ward.D',
           main = "Heatmap: Biomarker Discovery (Top 50 DE Peptides)")
} else {
  print("No significant peptides found to generate the Biomarker Resistance heatmap.")
}
```



```{r}
# 1. Filter for significance and deduplicate to unique proteins
sig_proteins_unique <- results_mech %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
  distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE)

print(paste("Analyzing", nrow(sig_proteins_unique), "unique significant proteins!"))

# 2. Convert IDs for clusterProfiler
converted <- bitr(sig_proteins_unique$Protein.Group, 
                  fromType = "UNIPROT", 
                  toType = "ENTREZID", 
                  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)

# 3. Gene Ontology (GO) Enrichment
ego <- enrichGO(gene = converted$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
                ont = "ALL", 
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                readable = TRUE)

dotplot(ego, showCategory = 10, title = "GO Enrichment: Mechanistic Stress")

# 4. KEGG Pathway Enrichment
ekegg <- enrichKEGG(gene = converted$ENTREZID,
                    organism = 'hsa',
                    pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 10, title = "KEGG Enrichment: Mechanistic Stress")
```
```{r}
# 1. Filter and Deduplicate
sig_baseline <- res_mech_baseline %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
  distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE)

if(nrow(sig_baseline) > 0) {
  # 2. Convert IDs
  conv_baseline <- bitr(sig_baseline$Protein.Group, fromType = "UNIPROT", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
  
  # 3. GO Enrichment
  ego_baseline <- enrichGO(gene = conv_baseline$ENTREZID, OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "ALL", pAdjustMethod = "BH", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, readable = TRUE)
  print(dotplot(ego_baseline, showCategory = 10, title = "GO: Mechanistic Baseline (c-Jun KO vs CTRL at 0h)"))
  
  # 4. KEGG Enrichment
  ekegg_baseline <- enrichKEGG(gene = conv_baseline$ENTREZID, organism = 'hsa', pvalueCutoff = 0.05)
  print(dotplot(ekegg_baseline, showCategory = 10, title = "KEGG: Mechanistic Baseline (c-Jun KO vs CTRL at 0h)"))
} else {
  print("No significant proteins found for Mechanistic Baseline pathway enrichment.")
}
```
```{r}
# 1. Filter and Deduplicate
sig_translation <- res_translation %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
  distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE)

if(nrow(sig_translation) > 0) {
  # 2. Convert IDs
  conv_translation <- bitr(sig_translation$Protein.Group, fromType = "UNIPROT", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
  
  # 3. GO Enrichment
  ego_translation <- enrichGO(gene = conv_translation$ENTREZID, OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "ALL", pAdjustMethod = "BH", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, readable = TRUE)
  print(dotplot(ego_translation, showCategory = 10, title = "GO: Translational Topic (JNK-IN-8 + Cisplatin vs Cisplatin)"))
  
  # 4. KEGG Enrichment
  ekegg_translation <- enrichKEGG(gene = conv_translation$ENTREZID, organism = 'hsa', pvalueCutoff = 0.05)
  print(dotplot(ekegg_translation, showCategory = 10, title = "KEGG: Translational Topic (JNK-IN-8 + Cisplatin vs Cisplatin)"))
} else {
  print("No significant proteins found for Translational Inhibitor pathway enrichment.")
}
```
```{r}
# 1. Filter and Deduplicate
sig_biomarker <- res_biomarker %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
  distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE)

if(nrow(sig_biomarker) > 0) {
  # 2. Convert IDs
  conv_biomarker <- bitr(sig_biomarker$Protein.Group, fromType = "UNIPROT", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
  
  # 3. GO Enrichment
  ego_biomarker <- enrichGO(gene = conv_biomarker$ENTREZID, OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "ALL", pAdjustMethod = "BH", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, readable = TRUE)
  print(dotplot(ego_biomarker, showCategory = 10, title = "GO: Biomarker Topic (CTRL Cisplatin vs CTRL Baseline)"))
  
  # 4. KEGG Enrichment
  ekegg_biomarker <- enrichKEGG(gene = conv_biomarker$ENTREZID, organism = 'hsa', pvalueCutoff = 0.05)
  print(dotplot(ekegg_biomarker, showCategory = 10, title = "KEGG: Biomarker Topic (CTRL Cisplatin vs CTRL Baseline)"))
} else {
  print("No significant proteins found for Biomarker Resistance pathway enrichment.")
}
```




## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
